---
title: "ACE Disparity Brief rundown"
author: "Caleb"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 90
---

# Background to idea for project

Three key aspects I wanted to explore relating to ancestral state estimation:

* how does fossil sampling influence estimate accuracy?
* how does this influence change across different rates of discrete trait evolution/models of continuous trait evolution.
* how does the 'threshold' value / 'fuzzy ace' for collapsing discrete states impact accuracy of ASE.


## First key enquiry: how accurate is ancestral state estimation?

There are three different parameters here:

* transition rate
* fossil sampling level
* method of collapsing ancestral states.

I measured the Hamming distance between true and 'aced' estimations. 


```{R echo = FALSE}
library(knitr)
## load the hamming data
relative_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointstrictmeans.rds")
no_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplestrictmeans.rds")
strict_majority_rule <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/threshstrictmeans.rds")

########################################################################################################################

## Load the disparity data
relvardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/relvardiffs.rds")
samplevardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/samplevardiffs.rds")
strictvardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/strictvardiffs.rds")

########################################################################################################################


```

```{R echo = FALSE, , warning = FALSE}

levels <- c("fast", "med", "slow")

# Loop through each level and generate a plot
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)


# Combine data for all levels and replicates into a single data frame
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Combine strict_means and strict_means_sample into a single data frame
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("strict_majority_rule", "relative_majority_rule", "no_majority_rule"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))
combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("strict_majority_rule", "relative_majority_rule", "no_majority_rule"),labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)"))


# png("../Manuscript/figures/strictdistance.png", width = 350, height = 250, units = "mm", res = 600)
colors <- c("slow" = "#8805A8", "med" = "#00B945", "fast" = "#FFB600")

# Generate the combined plot with faceting by threshold_method

colors <- c("slow" = "#8805A8", "med" = "#00B945", "fast" = "#FFB600")

# Generate the combined plot with violin plots
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 0.5,
    outlier.alpha = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil sampling",
    y = "Mean Hamming distance score",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  # Apply custom colors to lines
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +   # Apply custom colors to ribbons
  theme(
    # Thicker axis labels and ticks
    axis.text = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),      # Larger, bold axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),     # Larger, bold axis titles
    axis.ticks = element_line(size = 1),                   # Thicker axis ticks
    
    # Legend
    legend.position = "right",                 
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    
    panel.grid = element_blank(),             
    
    # Facet labels
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse(limits = c(1.0, 0.0), expand = c(0,0))  # Ensure 0 is included



```

This figure can go into the supplementary, as it does not ask the main question relating to disparity...



## Sum of variances

I then constructed distance matrices and ordinated both the 'true' and 'aced' matrices, then calculated the difference between the sum of variances values between true and aced.

```{R echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}

# discrete_var_default_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/pointvardiffs.rds")
# discrete_var_sample_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/samplevardiffs.rds")
# discrete_var_thresh_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/threshvardiffs.rds")
relvardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/relvardiffs.rds")
samplevardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/samplevardiffs.rds")
strictvardiffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/strictvardiffs.rds")
post_ord_diffs <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/discrete/post_ord_ace.Rds")

library(scales)
combined_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c("strictvardiffs", "relvardiffs", "samplevardiffs", "post_ord_diffs"), function(dataset_name) {
  dataset <- get(dataset_name)  # Dynamically get the dataset by name
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(dataset), function(level) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(dataset[[level]]), function(replicate_index) {
      data <- dataset[[level]][[replicate_index]]
      df <- data.frame(
        replicate = replicate_index,
        all = data$all,
        high = data$fossil_high,
        mid = data$fossil_med,
        low = data$fossil_low,
        living = data$living,
        level = level,
        threshold_method = dataset_name  # Add a column for treatment
      )
      df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
                              names_to = "preservation_level", values_to = "distance")
      df_long$preservation_level <- factor(df_long$preservation_level, levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"))
      return(df_long)
    }))
  }))
}))

combined_data$threshold_method <- factor(combined_data$threshold_method, levels = c("strictvardiffs", "relvardiffs", "samplevardiffs", "post_ord_diffs"), labels = c("Strict majority rule", "Relative majority rule", "No majority rule (sample)", "Post-ordination ace"))

# log10_reverse_trans <- trans_new(
#   name = "log10_reverse",
#   transform = function(x) -log10(x),  # Apply log10 and reverse
#   inverse = function(x) 10^(-x)      # Inverse of the transformation
# )

colors <- c("slow" = "#8805A8", "med" = "#00B945", "fast" = "#FFB600")
# Generate the combined plot
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = preservation_level, y = distance, color = level, fill = level)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.size = 0.5,
    outlier.alpha = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +  # Add horizontal line at y = 0
  facet_wrap(~ threshold_method, ncol = 4, nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil sampling",
    y = "Relative disparity error",
    color = "Transition Rate",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +  # Apply custom colors to lines
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) + 
  theme(
    # Thicker axis labels and ticks
    axis.text = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),      # Larger, bold axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),     # Larger, bold axis titles
    axis.ticks = element_line(size = 1),                   # Thicker axis ticks
    
    # Legend
    legend.position = "right",                 
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    
    panel.grid = element_blank(),             # Larger legend keys
    
    # Facet labels
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
    # plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10, unit = "pt")
      ) +
    scale_y_reverse()   # Match fill colors to line colors



```

## Random extinction

```{R echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
matched_strict <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/randomExtinction/matched.strict.Rds")
matched_rel <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/randomExtinction/matched.rel.Rds")
matched_sample <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/randomExtinction/matched.sample.Rds")
matched_no_ace <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/randomExtinction/matched.no_ace.Rds")




# Heat colors (red = high preservation, yellow = low)
library(viridis)
fossil_colors <- viridis(5, option = "plasma", direction = -1)
names(fossil_colors) <- c("all", "fossil_high", "fossil_med", "fossil_low", "living")


# # Terrain colors
# fossil_colors <- terrain.colors(5)
# names(fossil_colors) <- c("all", "fossil_high", "fossil_med", "fossil_low", "living")

# all_true <- c(matched_rel$true, matched_strict$true, matched_sample$true, matched_no_ace$true)
all_ace <- c(matched_rel$ace, matched_strict$ace, matched_sample$ace, matched_no_ace$ace)



# Create correlation plot
# Create the plot




# Create correlation plot
# Create the plot



# Create correlation plot
# Create the plot
plot(matched_sample$true, matched_sample$ace,
     xlab = "True Disparity Change",
     ylab = "ACED Disparity Change",  # Fixed label
     main = "Disparity Change: Sample ACE vs True",
     pch = 16, 
     col = fossil_colors[matched_sample$fossil_level],
     cex = 1,
     xlim = range(matched_rel$true),
     ylim = range(all_ace))

# Add 1:1 line
abline(0, 1, col = "black", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topleft", 
       legend = names(fossil_colors),
       col = fossil_colors,
       pch = 16,
       cex = 1,
       title = "Fossil Level")


## Note that for living no ace - there is no way that living taxa can be present during the mass extinction so it cannot be compared.


# Create the plot
plot(matched_no_ace$true, matched_no_ace$ace,
     xlab = "True Disparity Change",
     ylab = "ACED Disparity Change",  # Fixed label
     main = "Disparity Change: No ACE vs True",
     pch = 16, 
     col = fossil_colors[matched_no_ace$fossil_level],
     cex = 1,
     xlim = range(matched_rel$true),
     ylim = range(all_ace))

# Add 1:1 line
abline(0, 1, col = "black", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topleft", 
       legend = names(fossil_colors),
       col = fossil_colors,
       pch = 16,
       cex = 1,
       title = "Fossil Level")





```

## Selective extinction

### Sample ace selective extinction

```{R echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
matched_strict <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/selectiveExtinction/matched.strict.Rds")
matched_rel <- readRDS("../Data/matrices/selectiveExtinction/matched.rel.Rds")
matched_sample <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/selectiveExtinction/matched.sample.Rds")
matched_no_ace <- readRDS( "../Data/matrices/selectiveExtinction/matched.no_ace.Rds")




# Heat colors (red = high preservation, yellow = low)
library(viridis)
fossil_colors <- viridis(5, option = "plasma", direction = -1)
names(fossil_colors) <- c("all", "fossil_high", "fossil_med", "fossil_low", "living")


# # Terrain colors
# fossil_colors <- terrain.colors(5)
# names(fossil_colors) <- c("all", "fossil_high", "fossil_med", "fossil_low", "living")

# all_true <- c(matched_rel$true, matched_strict$true, matched_sample$true, matched_no_ace$true)
all_ace <- c(matched_rel$ace, matched_strict$ace, matched_sample$ace, matched_no_ace$ace)



# Create correlation plot
# Create the plot




# Create correlation plot
# Create the plot



# # Create correlation plot
# # Create the plot
# plot(matched_sample$true, matched_sample$ace,
#      xlab = "True Disparity Change",
#      ylab = "ACED Disparity Change",  # Fixed label
#      main = "Disparity Change: Sample ACE vs True",
#      pch = 16, 
#      col = fossil_colors[matched_sample$fossil_level],
#      cex = 1,
#      xlim = range(matched_rel$true),
#      ylim = range(all_ace))

# # Add 1:1 line
# abline(0, 1, col = "black", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
# legend("topleft", 
#        legend = names(fossil_colors),
#        col = fossil_colors,
#        pch = 16,
#        cex = 1,
#        title = "Fossil Level")


# ## Note that for living no ace - there is no way that living taxa can be present during the mass extinction so it cannot be compared.


# # Create the plot
# plot(matched_no_ace$true, matched_no_ace$ace,
#      xlab = "True Disparity Change",
#      ylab = "ACED Disparity Change",  # Fixed label
#      main = "Disparity Change: No ACE vs True",
#      pch = 16, 
#      col = fossil_colors[matched_no_ace$fossil_level],
#      cex = 1,
#      xlim = range(matched_rel$true),
#      ylim = range(all_ace))

# # Add 1:1 line
# abline(0, 1, col = "black", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
# legend("topleft", 
#        legend = names(fossil_colors),
#        col = fossil_colors,
#        pch = 16,
#        cex = 1,
#        title = "Fossil Level")





knitr::include_graphics(path = "../Manuscript/figures/selectiveExtinction/correlation/sample_disp_change.png")
```


### No ace selective extinction

```{r echo = FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics(path = "../Manuscript/figures/selectiveExtinction/correlation/no_ace_disp_change.png")


```

### Other disparity metrics

* Sum of ranges
* Average displacement
* Some sort of density metric
